title: THE FOUR OLDS — sc02 the bank, table read

beats.caption: INT. FIRST PLAINS BANK - LOAN OFFICE - DAY

panel.gen: Small-town bank loan office, a young loan officer in a too-new tie on a desk phone behind a cluttered desk, an old farmer in a pressed white shirt buttoned to the collar sitting across from him holding a squared manila folder on his knees, fluorescent light, venetian blinds, a cardboard promotional sign on an easel in the background.

lines.text: [calm] First Plains Bank, Broken Bow, Nebraska. Fluorescent light, carpet tiles. A cardboard sign by the door: The Stewardship Act — working for communities like yours. Nobody is looking at it. Cricket Dawes, seventy-nine, waits in a pressed white shirt, a manila folder square on his knees. In the loan office, the officer is on the phone.

[distracted, wrapping up a call] — no, the culvert's the county's. That's not ours, tell him to call Roads. Roads. R-O— I have to go. [exhales, recovering] Mr. Dawes. Sorry. Everybody's got a flooded something this week.
Cricket opens the folder. Tab dividers, fifty years of order. He slides the county letter across.
[polite, already typing] I've got it on screen, you don't need—
[dry] Cricket slides the assessment appeal receipt across too. The officer takes it to be polite and sets it down without reading it.
[level, unhurried] County reassessed in March. Four thousand two hundred six dollars, due the fifteenth. I have two thousand one hundred.

[typing, hopeful] Okay. So there was a program for exactly this. Hardship deferral on ag-adjacent parcels, it's — hang on. [reading, losing the thread] "Suspended pending alignment review of county remittance instruments under the" — um. It's paused. Not paused — there's no — there isn't a mechanism right now. Is what that means.
[flat] When does it come back?
[quietly defeated] It doesn't say. It's federal now. The language doesn't — it doesn't say.
[quiet] A silence. The officer scrolls Cricket's accounts, filling it.

[making small talk, then puzzled] You've banked here — wow. Sixty-one years. That's longer than my dad's been alive. [squinting] There's a deposit here from NASA. Every quarter. For zero dollars and zero cents. That's got to be a glitch, I can put a ticket in and get it scrubbed off your—
[sharp, too fast] Leave it.
[hushed] Too fast. Too hard. The officer looks up. Cricket sits exactly as he was, level again.
[quiet, even] It's been there fifty years. It can stay.
[carefully] ...Sure.

beats.caption: INT. FIRST PLAINS BANK - LOAN OFFICE - DAY

lines.text: He closes that window. Sits back. Thinks. Then he pulls a form from the drawer and talks while he fills it in, not looking up.

[casual, working, not looking up] What I can do is — our scanner's been down, there's a whole tray of stuff that hasn't been logged, things get received when they get received — [a stamp thunks] — so your paperwork came in next cycle. That's sixty days before anything goes delinquent. Don't tell my branch manager. [beat, wry] Or do, honestly. She does it too.
Cricket looks at the stamp. Puts the copy in the folder, in order, and stands.
[plain] What do I owe you?
[deadpan, then catching his own joke] It's a bank, Mr. Dawes. You owe us four thousand dollars.
A beat. Almost a smile out of Cricket. He stops at the door, half turned.

[offhand, practical] Your culvert caller. If that's the one under Route 12 by the Hollen place, that's county, he's right. But Roads won't get to it till October. Log chain and a tractor, some Saturday. Hour of work.
[a beat, quietly impressed] ...I'll pass that on.

beats.caption: EXT. FIRST PLAINS BANK, PARKING LOT - DAY

panel.gen: View from inside an old pickup truck cab, an elderly farmer's weathered hands resting on the steering wheel with keys, through the windshield a small-town courthouse square with an American flag on a tall pole against the sky, manila folder on the bench seat.

lines.text: [slow] Cricket sits in his pickup, folder on the bench seat, keys in his hand. Through the windshield: the courthouse across the square, and above it, the flag, moving. [beat] He watches it one moment. He starts the truck.
